I’ll continue to cover the second Tuesday release here and update this blog should the fourth Tuesday release contain anything significant.
The patch for Cold Fusion even clocks in with a CVSS 9.9 bug.
There are patches for Windows and Windows components, Office and Office Components, Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based), Azure, .NET and Visual Studio, Github Copilot, Defender, Exchange Server, Hyper-V, Ages of Empire II, and Minecraft Server (really!).
CVE-2026-50522 was demonstrated during Pwn2Own Berlin, so it’s odd to see Microsoft list it as “Exploit Maturity Unknown” since we literally handed them a working exploit.
This bug is a heap-based buffer overflow in Minecraft Bedrock Dedicated Server, also CVSS 9.8 and also unauthenticated RCE.

tavily.raw: {"query":"CVE-2026-34743 xz utils patched version fix release CVSS score","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://beyondmachines.net/event_details/xz-utils-5-8-3-released-to-patch-buffer-overflow-and-memory-access-flaws-8-i-t-m-h","title":"XZ Utils 5.8.3 Released to Patch Buffer Overflow and Memory Access Flaws","content":"3. XZ Utils 5.8.3 Released to Patch Buffer Overflow and Memory Access Flaws. Take action: Even if developers claim a bug is hard to hit, a CVSS 9.8 in a core library like XZ requires attention. The developers of XZ Utils have released version 5.8.3 to address two security vulnerabilities, including a critically scord buffer overflow that affects the core library. The update affects all versions of the compression library since 5.0.0, making it a significant concern for Linux distributions and software that relies on liblzma for data compression. * **CVE-2026-34743 (CVSS score 9.8) -** A buffer overflow in the `lzma_index_append()` function that occurs when an application attempts to append records to a decoded index that contains no records. A successful exploit of the buffer overflow could allow for remote code execution if an attacker can control the index data being processed by the library. The vulnerabilities impact XZ Utils versions 5.0.0 through 5.8.2 across various Linux distributions and operating systems.","score":0.89189607,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://feedly.com/cve/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743 - Exploits & Severity - Feedly","content":"# CVE-2026-34743. Published: Apr 2, 2026/ Updated: 3mo ago. XZ Utils versions prior to 5.8.3 contain a heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the lzma\\_index\\_decoder() function. This could result in a buffer overflow that may lead to denial of service through application crashes or potential code execution, depending on the application's memory layout and protections. There is no evidence of proof of exploitation at the moment. Update XZ Utils to version 5.8.3 or later. 1. Prioritize upgrading all XZ Utils installations to version 5.8.3 or later. 2. Inventory systems running XZ Utils versions prior to 5.8.3 and apply patches immediately. Feedly estimated the CVSS as HIGHbased on the CVE details, attack complexity, and exploit information.Learn more. Feedly estimated the CVSS as MEDIUMbased on the CVE details, attack complexity, and exploit information.Learn more. NVD published the first details for CVE-2026-34743. Impact summary: The stack buffer overflow or NULL pointer dereference may cause a crash leading to Denial of Service for an application that parses untrusted PKCS#12 files.","score":0.8849276,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.heise.de/en/news/XZ-Utils-5-8-3-Security-update-with-unclear-risk-11244670.html","title":"XZ Utils 5.8.3: Security update with unclear risk","content":"## heise+ Das Beste aus heise+. ## heise online Top-Beiträge von heise online. ## heise online Most popular articles by heise online. # XZ Utils 5.8.3: Security update with unclear risk. The developers of the widely used XZ Utils have released an updated version that patches security vulnerabilities. Version 5.8.3 of XZ Utils patches security vulnerabilities, among other things. The release announcement for XZ Utils 5.8.3 lists the changes. It fixes a vulnerability in the `lzma_index_append()` function, which can lead to a buffer overflow – \"under conditions that likely don't exist in any real-world application\", as the programmers write (CVE-2026-34743). The updated version of XZ Utils also corrects a problem that can lead to memory access in areas not intended for it – at least on 32-bit systems and with some preconditions. The security vulnerability CVE-2026-34743 affects XZ Utils from version 5.0.0 onwards. IT managers should check for the availability of updated XZ Util packages in their deployed distributions and apply them promptly.","score":0.8396422,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743: XZ Utils Buffer Overflow Vulnerability","content":"# CVE-2026-34743: XZ Utils Buffer Overflow Vulnerability. CVE-2026-34743 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in XZ Utils affecting lzma\\_index\\_decoder() function. The vulnerability exists in the lzma\\_index\\_decoder() function when decoding an Index that contains no Records. When this condition occurs, the resulting lzma\\_index structure is left in an invalid state where a subsequent call to lzma\\_index\\_append() allocates insufficient memory, leading to a heap buffer overflow. > Applications using XZ Utils that decode empty Index structures and subsequently append Records may be vulnerable to heap buffer overflow, potentially leading to denial of service or memory corruption. When an application subsequently calls lzma\\_index\\_append() after decoding an empty Index, the function allocates a buffer based on the incorrect prealloc value of zero, resulting in insufficient memory allocation and a subsequent heap buffer overflow when data is written. The root cause is a missing boundary check in the preallocation logic within src/liblzma/common/index.c. When lzma\\_index\\_decoder() is called with records == 0, the code failed to set a safe default value for i->prealloc, leaving it at zero.","score":0.78532547,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-34743","title":"CVE-2026-34743 Detail - NVD","content":"# You are viewing this page in an unauthorized frame window.
